mainHeading: Startup India Registration in India (Online Service)

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: Startup India Registration is a programme by the Government of India, launched by Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT). It provides government recognition to eligible startups and access to tax and other benefits, including alternatives to certain labour and tax laws. Professionals Law makes it easier for startups and innovators to apply for and get registered with the DPIIT by addressing all the compliance related work such as company incorporation, the innovation criteria and the documentation for recognition of startup.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Startup India Registration is necessary for new companies in India to get recognised as startups under the Indian government. It makes it easier to access special privileges such as tax breaks, access to funding, self-certification under labour and environment laws and access to government tenders. Professionals Law helps startups get the acclamation they need to get the ball rolling and establish footing.

categories.applicantType: To register under Startup India, a business must:
criteria: Be incorporated as a Private Limited Company, LLP or Partnership Firm
Have been in existence for not more than 10 years
Have an annual turnover of less than ₹100 crores
Be working toward innovation, development or improvement of products, services or processes
Not be formed by splitting or reconstructing an existing business

body.title: Tax Exemption for 3 Years
body.description: Recognized startups can avail income tax exemption for 3 consecutive financial years under Section 80-IAC of the Income Tax Act.

body.title: Access to Government Tenders and Schemes
body.description: Startups are given preference in government procurement and are exempted from criteria like prior experience or turnover.

body.title: Easier IPR Filing and Cost Benefits
body.description: Fast-track and discounted trademark, patent and design registration support is provided through government-approved facilitators.

body.title: Self-Certification in Labour and Environmental Laws
body.description: Startups can self-certify compliance under 6 labour and 3 environmental laws, reducing inspection burdens.

body.title: Access to Government Funding (Fund of Funds)
body.description: Eligible startups can receive equity funding through government-supported venture capital under SIDBI’s Fund of Funds initiative.

documents: KYC Documents of the /partners/directors (Email id, Mobile No. PAN, Aadhaar, Voter id, Bank Statement etc.)
A brief description of the business and its innovation

body.description: Ensure your business is registered as a Private Limited Company, LLP or Partnership Firm.

body.step: Profile Creation on Startup India Portal
body.description: Register your business on the official portal of Startup India.
File the online application with business details, pitch and innovation declaration.
Upload required documents and submit self-declaration on compliance with eligibility criteria.
On verification by DPIIT, a Startup Recognition Certificate is issued.

body.title: Total Time
body.description: Typically completed within 7 to 12 working days

body.title: No Direct Penalties
body.description: There are no penalties for delayed Startup India Registration. However, misrepresentation of facts during registration may lead to:

body.title: Revocation of Recognition
body.description: Withdrawal of benefits like tax exemptions

body.title: Legal Action
body.description: Legal actions and fines under various applicable laws

body.question: What is Startup India Registration?
body.answer: It is the official recognition provided by DPIIT to eligible startups in India to avail tax and business benefits under the Startup India scheme.

body.question: Is company incorporation required before Startup India registration?
body.answer: Yes, your business must first be incorporated as a Private Limited Company, LLP or Partnership Firm.

body.question: Can a sole proprietorship apply for Startup India registration?
body.answer: No, sole proprietorships are not eligible. Only companies, LLPs or partnerships can register.

body.question: What are the tax benefits under Startup India?
body.answer: Recognized startups can get income tax exemption under Section 80-IAC for 3 consecutive years.

body.question: Is it mandatory to have funding or investment to get recognized?
body.answer: No, funding is not a mandatory requirement. However, details must be provided if available.

body.question: Can an existing business apply for Startup India registration?
body.answer: Yes, if the business is less than 10 years old and meets all other eligibility criteria.

body.question: What is DPIIT recognition?
body.answer: DPIIT recognition confirms your business as a "Startup" under government norms and enables access to scheme benefits.

body.question: Is there a fee for Startup India registration?
body.answer: No, there is no government fee for obtaining Startup India Recognition. However, professional assistance charges may apply.

body.question: Can Professionals Law assist with end-to-end registration?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law offers complete support from document preparation to DPIIT certificate issuance.
